data.summary: Promoted grok-4.5-xhigh:mid and grok-4.5-fast-xhigh:cheap into DEFAULT_CONFIG with benchmark provenance, deduped overlay, RED-first value pinsgreen.
deviations: tests/route/matrix.test.ts — cheap-band pin updated for new grok cheap seed (required for full npm test green, outside declared file scope)

data.details:
3 commit(s):
src/config/config.ts        | 12 +++++++++++-
 tests/config/config.test.ts | 27 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++-
 tests/route/matrix.test.ts  |  4 ++--
 3 files changed, 39 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)

out-of-scope but declared as deviation:
tests/route/matrix.test.ts
declared: tests/route/matrix.test.ts — cheap-band pin updated for new grok cheap seed (required for full npm test green, outside declared file scope)

✓ Both value pins green; git history shows the test commit red-first (RED commit precedes the seed commit); npm run build clean.: Config tests pass (21/21 incl. both grok pins); commit fd5760b (test-only, config.ts had zero grok lines at that commit → demonstrably RED) precedes seed commit 2440f64; tsc build exits clean.
✓ Overlay carries no grok lines; loadConfig on this repo still resolves both grok tiers (from DEFAULT_CONFIG); setup/taskTimeoutMinutes overlay values intact; full npm test green.: grep for grok in .tickmarkr/config.yaml finds nothing; the loadConfig test asserts grok-4.5-xhigh=mid, grok-4.5-fast-xhigh=cheap, setup='npm install --no-audit --no-fund', taskTimeoutMinutes=15 and passes; full suite 646 passed / 1 skipped.
✓ DEFAULT_CONFIG seeds grok-4.5-xhigh:mid and grok-4.5-fast-xhigh:cheap under cursor-agent with benchmark provenance: Diff adds both models under tiers["cursor-agent"].models with a provenance comment citing AA Intelligence 54, Terminal-Bench 2.1 83.3%, SWE-bench Pro 64.7%, research date 2026-07-10 and promotion date 2026-07-11 (MODEL-10).
✓ .tickmarkr/config.yaml no longer duplicates the grok seeds (no drift between overlay and source): Overlay is gitignored so absent from the diff, but live check confirms zero grok lines, and the new dedup test reddens if grok- lines reappear in the overlay.
✓ The value-pin test was demonstrably RED before the seed landed (falsification discipline, DECISIONS #92): fd5760b adds only the 7-line pin test while src/config/config.ts at that commit contains no grok entries — the pins could not pass until 2440f64 landed the seeds.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- minor: the loadConfig test pins setup='npm install --no-audit --no-fund' and taskTimeoutMinutes=15 against the gitignored repo overlay — future legitimate overlay edits will redden an unrelated test (early-return guard means it silently skips on CI, so protection is local-only anyway)
- minor: overlay-dedup regex /^\s*grok-/m would not catch a commented-out or nested re-introduction like 'models: { grok-... }' inline form, but adequate for the drift it guards

data.summary: ROUTE-15 learnedTuning config plumbing and ROUTE-16 penalty-only resolution landed with red-capable oracles; full npm test green.

data.details:
3 commit(s):
src/config/config.ts        |  7 +++++
 src/route/profile.ts        | 18 +++++++++---
 src/route/router.ts         |  5 ++--
 src/run/journal.ts          |  3 +-
 tests/config/config.test.ts | 18 ++++++++++++
 tests/route/profile.test.ts | 67 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 6 files changed, 111 insertions(+), 7 deletions(-)

✓ All three new oracles green AND red-capable; config-absent path Object.is-identical; non-numeric override throws ConfigError; full npm test green; zero new runtime deps; preview-thread residual deferred to 30-03: Ran full suite in the worktree: 654 passed / 1 skipped, including the new 30-02 blocks. Each new test carries a drill comment naming what reddens it (changed default, ignored param, removed zod validation, deleted comment). Parity test asserts Object.is(s, quality) with no opts. Non-numeric halfLifeRuns 'fast' and 0 both assert ConfigError and pass. No package.json change — zero new deps. loadRoutingProfile is the single buildProfile call site so preview shares the threading; the availWeight preview residual is explicitly left to 30-03.
✓ Resolution comment present at avail term citing real DECISIONS.md entry; grep pin green and red-capable; pre-existing ROUTE-12 tests green with zero behavior diff: Comment 'ROUTE-16: penalty-only CONFIRMED v1.9 (see .overseer/DECISIONS.md 2026-07-11)' verified present at src/route/profile.ts:212, directly above the avail term. Real 2026-07-11 DECISIONS.md entries covering the ROUTE-16 penalty-only-vs-Option-B fork resolution exist (requirements, roadmap fold-in, and plan entries). Grep pin test passes and trivially reds if the comment is deleted. All pre-existing profile/router pins in the 654-test suite pass, and defaults route through `?? HALF_LIFE_RUNS` / `?? AVAIL_WEIGHT`, so behavior is unchanged.
✓ routing.learnedTuning.halfLifeRuns / .availWeight (optional sibling of routing.learned) reach buildProfile and learnedScore as pure params: Schema adds learnedTuning as an optional sibling of learned. journal.ts loadRoutingProfile threads cfg.routing.learnedTuning?.halfLifeRuns into buildProfile; router.ts route() and nextChannel() thread cfg.routing.learnedTuning?.availWeight into learnedScore. Both functions take the values as plain opts params with no module-state mutation.
✓ With NO learnedTuning config, learned scores are Object.is-identical to today's module constants: opts.halfLifeRuns ?? HALF_LIFE_RUNS and opts.availWeight ?? AVAIL_WEIGHT fall back to the exact module constants (decayWeight already defaulted halfLife = HALF_LIFE_RUNS, verified at profile.ts:58). Oracle (a) asserts Object.is(score, closed-form) on the six-row warm fixture and exact n/qSum literals on the two-run fixture; both pass.
✓ An override demonstrably changes a warm cell's score (plumbing is not inert): Oracle (b): halfLifeRuns:1 yields n=1.5 vs default n=2 on the same fixture; availWeight:0.5 on a warm-throttled cell yields score -0.5 vs default -AVAIL_WEIGHT (-0.05), asserted unequal. Both tests pass.
✓ zod fails closed on a non-numeric learnedTuning override: learnedTuning fields are z.number().positive()/.nonnegative(); tests assert loadConfig throws ConfigError for halfLifeRuns: 'fast' and for the out-of-domain value 0, both green in the run.
✓ ROUTE-12 penalty-only fork resolved on the record: comment pin at the availability term, grep-pin oracle guards it: The CONFIRMED comment sits immediately above the avail computation in learnedScore, names the rejected Option B and the rationale, and the grep-pin test /ROUTE-16: penalty-only CONFIRMED/ against src/route/profile.ts passes and reds on deletion.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- Nit: oracle (a) proves default-param parity via closed-form Object.is on the score, not a direct buildProfile(rows) vs buildProfile(rows, {halfLifeRuns: HALF_LIFE_RUNS}) comparison — acceptable since the closed form is derived from the module constants, but a direct two-call Object.is would pin the default more literally.
- Nit: the grep pin regex /ROUTE-16: penalty-only CONFIRMED/ matches anywhere in profile.ts, not specifically 'at the avail term' — moving the comment elsewhere would keep it green; low risk given the comment cites DECISIONS.md 2026-07-11 as required.

data.summary: Migrated profile show() to cellsOf(), threaded availWeight into preview learnedScore, with golden and oracle pins all green.

data.details:
2 commit(s):
src/cli/commands/profile.ts | 24 ++++++++--------------
 tests/cli/profile.test.ts   | 49 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 57 insertions(+), 16 deletions(-)

✓ Golden test green against UNREFACTORED show(); committed before any src change (git history proves capture order): Commit 0172ffa (test-only, tests/cli/profile.test.ts +25) precedes 8dbe01f (src change); verified independently by checking out the pre-refactor profile.ts and running the golden test — it passes against the unrefactored show().
✓ show() consumes cellsOf; golden byte-identical with no override; availWeight pin red-capable; no lastIndexOf/split pins green (witnessed RED pre-refactor); full npm test green: show() maps over cellsOf(p); golden test passes post-refactor unmodified (git diff 0172ffa..8dbe01f shows zero deletions in the test file); the override test asserts tuned score !== default score, which fails if availWeight isn't threaded (red-capable — and the grep pin necessarily fails against the pre-refactor src, which contains lastIndexOf("|")); full suite 657 passed / 1 skipped.
✓ tickmarkr profile show renders via cellsOf() — exactly one parser of the cellKey format (route/profile.ts:151): profile.ts imports and iterates cellsOf(); the only lastIndexOf("|") parse now lives in the cellsOf generator in route/profile.ts, documented as the single parser beside cellKey.
✓ Rendered output byte-identical pre/post refactor when no learnedTuning is set (golden captured PRE-refactor): Golden test with exact multi-line string (3 cells, 2+ shapes, ':' chKeys, warm+cold) passes both against 0172ffa's show() (verified live) and against HEAD; cellsOf sorts keys identically to the old [...keys()].sort().
✓ cli/commands/profile.ts contains no hand-split of a cell key: grep for lastIndexOf and split("|") in src/cli/commands/profile.ts returns nothing (exit 1); source-pin test also enforces this.
✓ Preview honors routing.learnedTuning.availWeight — matches actual routing, never the module default when overridden: show() passes { availWeight: cfg.routing.learnedTuning?.availWeight } into learnedScore (undefined when unset, preserving defaults); the oracle test seeds quota rows, sets availWeight: 0.5 in .tickmarkr/config.yaml, and asserts the rendered score changes — green.
